tasks.spec: Perform a fresh, independent, read-only admission gate for Bead hermes-jxk0. Do not mutate Paperclip, Beads, repositories, services, or configuration. Query the live Paperclip API using the absolute executable /usr/bin/curl at http://127.0.0.1:3100/api for company 87c32b8e-f131-4df8-ad8e-963d01b458e7. Inspect current issues plus enough agent/run state to decide whether ten DISTINCT agent-safe Paperclip issues can be executed consecutively now. Write exactly one artifact named streak-gate.md. It must contain these exact headings: # Verdict, ## Live evidence, ## Eligibility rules, ## Sequential execution protocol, ## Reset conditions, ## Receipt schema, ## Current candidate assessment, ## Safe next action. Immediately after # Verdict, state exactly ADMIT or HOLD on its own nonblank line. ADMIT is allowed only if at least ten distinct currently actionable, non-personal, non-running issues with safe autonomous completion paths are proven live. Include these exact literal contract phrases somewhere in the artifact: one-at-a-time; pre/post live read-back; successful terminal run state; done issue state; zero adapter/runtime errors; reset to zero; issue UUID; run UUID; sequence number; personal/human-owned; blocked issues; already-running; synthetic repetitions; duplicate IDs. The sequential protocol must require immutable evidence and one-at-a-time execution. The reset rule must reset the entire streak to zero after any error. The receipt schema must include issue UUID, identifier, agent UUID, run UUID, start/end timestamps, terminal state, exit code, error reason, evidence path, and sequence number. Exclude personal/human-owned work, blocked issues, already-running work, synthetic repetitions, duplicates, and unsafe or approval-gated work. Never print or store credentials. Do not claim task execution occurred; this is only an admission gate. Be conservative and factual.
tasks.check:
set -eu; f=streak-gate.md; test -s "$f" || { echo 'FAIL: streak-gate.md missing or empty' >&2; exit 1; }; for heading in '# Verdict' '## Live evidence' '## Eligibility rules' '## Sequential execution protocol' '## Reset conditions' '## Receipt schema' '## Current candidate assessment' '## Safe next action'; do grep -Fxq -- "$heading" "$f" || { printf 'FAIL: missing exact heading: %s
' "$heading" >&2; exit 1; }; done; for term in 'one-at-a-time' 'pre/post live read-back' 'successful terminal run state' 'done issue state' 'zero adapter/runtime errors' 'reset to zero' 'issue UUID' 'run UUID' 'sequence number' 'personal/human-owned' 'blocked issues' 'already-running' 'synthetic repetitions' 'duplicate IDs'; do grep -Fiq -- "$term" "$f" || { printf 'FAIL: missing required contract phrase: %s
' "$term" >&2; exit 1; }; done; verdict=$(awk '/^# Verdict$/{getline; while ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*$/) getline; print; exit}' "$f"); case "$verdict" in ADMIT|HOLD) ;; *) printf 'FAIL: verdict must be ADMIT or HOLD, found: %s
' "$verdict" >&2; exit 1;; esac; bytes=$(wc -c < "$f" | tr -d ' '); test "$bytes" -ge 3000 || { printf 'FAIL: streak gate too small: %s bytes
' "$bytes" >&2; exit 1; }; printf 'EXECUTED_CHECK=PASS live-protocol-gate verdict=%s bytes=%s
' "$verdict" "$bytes"
tasks.verified: A fresh independent live gate applied the strict ten-distinct-task Paperclip admission and reset contract.
